quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 7, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Stoichiometry: Mass, Moles, Particles, and Gas Volumes
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Stoichiometric Calculations, Limiting Reactants, Percent Yield, Gas Volumes at STP, Particle Counts, Mass-Mole-Particle Conversions

questions.question: Sn + 2HCl â†’ SnClâ‚‚ + Hâ‚‚. If 0.8 moles of SnClâ‚‚ are produced, how many grams of Sn (molar mass 118.71 g/mol) were reacted?
questions.explanation:
Mole ratio Sn:SnClâ‚‚ = 1:1
Moles Sn reacted = 0.8 mol
Mass Sn = 0.8 أ— 118.71 = 94.97 g â‰ˆ 95.0 g

questions.question: Pâ‚„ + 10Clâ‚‚ â†’ 4PClâ‚…. If 0.14 moles of Clâ‚‚ are reacted with 7.45 grams of Pâ‚„, how many particles of PClâ‚… will be produced?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Pâ‚„ = 123.9 g/mol
Moles Pâ‚„ = 7.45/123.9 = 0.0601 mol
From Pâ‚„: need Clâ‚‚ = 0.0601 أ— 10 = 0.601 mol (have 0.14 mol) â†’ Clâ‚‚ limiting
PClâ‚… from Clâ‚‚ = 0.14 أ— (4/10) = 0.056 mol
Particles = 0.056 أ— 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 3.37 أ— 10آ²آ²

questions.question: PbO + C â†’ Pb + CO. If 1.81 أ— 10آ²âپ´ particles of PbO are reacted with 9.76 moles of C, how many grams of CO will be produced?
questions.explanation:
Moles PbO = 1.81 أ— 10آ²âپ´ / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 3.01 mol
1:1 ratio, need 3.01 mol C (have 9.76 mol) â†’ PbO limiting
CO produced = 3.01 mol
Molar mass CO = 28.01 g/mol
Mass = 3.01 أ— 28.01 = 84.3 g

questions.question: 2Hâ‚‚ + Oâ‚‚ â†’ 2Hâ‚‚O. When 15.50 grams of Hâ‚‚ (molar mass 2.02 g/mol) are reacted with 3.250 moles of Oâ‚‚, 4.875 moles of Hâ‚‚O are produced. What is the percent yield of this reaction?
questions.explanation:
Moles Hâ‚‚ = 15.50/2.02 = 7.673 mol
Need Oâ‚‚ for 7.673 mol Hâ‚‚ = 7.673/2 = 3.836 mol (have 3.250 mol) â†’ Oâ‚‚ limiting
Theoretical Hâ‚‚O = 3.250 أ— 2 = 6.50 mol
Actual Hâ‚‚O = 4.875 mol
Percent yield = (4.875/6.50) أ— 100% = 75.0%

questions.question: CaCOâ‚ƒ â†’ CaO + COâ‚‚. If 0.75 moles of CaCOâ‚ƒ are reacted, how many liters of COâ‚‚ will be produced at STP if the reaction proceeds with a 72% yield?
questions.explanation:
Theoretical COâ‚‚ = 0.75 mol
Actual COâ‚‚ = 0.75 أ— 0.72 = 0.54 mol
Volume at STP = 0.54 أ— 22.4 = 12.1 L

questions.question: 2FeO + 2Clâ‚‚ â†’ 2FeClâ‚‚ + Oâ‚‚. If 7.6 أ— 10آ²âپ´ particles of Clâ‚‚ are reacted with 920 grams of FeO (molar mass 71.84 g/mol), how many liters of Oâ‚‚ will be produced at STP?
questions.explanation:
Moles Clâ‚‚ = 7.6 أ— 10آ²âپ´ / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 12.62 mol
Moles FeO = 920/71.84 = 12.80 mol
Need FeO for 12.62 mol Clâ‚‚ = 12.62 mol (have 12.80 mol) â†’ Clâ‚‚ limiting
Oâ‚‚ produced = 12.62/2 = 6.31 mol
Volume at STP = 6.31 أ— 22.4 = 141 L

questions.question: 2Alâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ + 3C â†’ 4Al + 3COâ‚‚. If 5.86 أ— 10آ²آ² particles of C are reacted, how many grams of Alâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ (molar mass 101.96 g/mol) will also be reacted?
questions.explanation:
Moles C = 5.86 أ— 10آ²آ² / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 0.0973 mol
Mole ratio C:Alâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ = 3:2
Moles Alâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ = 0.0973 أ— (2/3) = 0.0649 mol
Mass Alâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ = 0.0649 أ— 101.96 = 6.62 g

questions.question: 2C + Hâ‚‚ â†’ Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚. If 2.3 أ— 10آ²âپ´ particles of Hâ‚‚ are reacted with 0.052 moles of C, how many moles of Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚ will be produced?
questions.explanation:
Moles Hâ‚‚ = 2.3 أ— 10آ²âپ´ / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 3.82 mol
Mole ratio C:Hâ‚‚:Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚ = 2:1:1
For 0.052 mol C, need Hâ‚‚ = 0.052/2 = 0.026 mol (have 3.82 mol) â†’ C is limiting
Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚ produced = 0.052/2 = 0.026 mol

questions.question: 2NaCl + Fâ‚‚ â†’ 2NaF + Clâ‚‚. If 1.02 liters of Fâ‚‚ are reacted with 3.3 أ— 10آ²âپ´ particles of NaCl, how many grams of Clâ‚‚ (molar mass 70.91 g/mol) will be produced at STP?
questions.explanation:
Moles Fâ‚‚ = 1.02 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.0455 mol
Moles NaCl = 3.3 أ— 10آ²âپ´ / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 5.48 mol
For 0.0455 mol Fâ‚‚, need NaCl = 0.0455 أ— 2 = 0.0910 mol (have 5.48 mol) â†’ Fâ‚‚ limiting
Mole ratio Fâ‚‚:Clâ‚‚ = 1:1, so Clâ‚‚ = 0.0455 mol
Mass Clâ‚‚ = 0.0455 أ— 70.91 = 3.23 g

questions.question: 2Feâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ + 3C â†’ 4Fe + 3COâ‚‚. If 420 grams of Feâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ (molar mass 159.69 g/mol) are reacted, how many grams of COâ‚‚ (molar mass 44.01 g/mol) will be produced if the reaction proceeds with a 65.8% yield?
questions.explanation:
Moles Feâ‚‚Oâ‚ƒ = 420/159.69 = 2.63 mol
Theoretical COâ‚‚ = 2.63 أ— (3/2) = 3.945 mol
Mass theoretical COâ‚‚ = 3.945 أ— 44.01 = 173.6 g
65.8% yield â†’ actual COâ‚‚ = 173.6 أ— 0.658 = 114 g

questions.question: CO + CHâ‚ƒOH â†’ CHâ‚ƒCOOH. When 5.823 أ— 10آ²âپµ particles of CHâ‚ƒCOOH are produced, 6.041 أ— 10آ²âپµ particles of CHâ‚ƒOH were reacted. What is the percent yield of this reaction?
questions.explanation:
Moles CHâ‚ƒOH reacted = 6.041 أ— 10آ²âپµ / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 100.3 mol
Theoretical CHâ‚ƒCOOH = 100.3 mol (1:1 ratio)
Actual CHâ‚ƒCOOH = 5.823 أ— 10آ²âپµ / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 96.7 mol
Percent yield = (96.7/100.3) أ— 100% = 96.4%

questions.question: 2KBr + Clâ‚‚ â†’ 2KCl + Brâ‚‚. If 132.6 grams of Clâ‚‚ (molar mass 70.91 g/mol) are reacted with 38.4 grams of KBr (molar mass 119 g/mol), how many particles of KCl will be produced if the reaction proceeds with a 88.6% yield?
questions.explanation:
Moles Clâ‚‚ = 132.6/70.91 = 1.87 mol
Moles KBr = 38.4/119 = 0.323 mol
Need Clâ‚‚ for 0.323 mol KBr = 0.323/2 = 0.1615 mol (have 1.87 mol) â†’ KBr limiting
Theoretical KCl = 0.323 mol
88.6% yield â†’ actual KCl = 0.323 أ— 0.886 = 0.286 mol
Particles KCl = 0.286 أ— 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 1.72 أ— 10آ²آ³

questions.question: Nâ‚‚ + 2Oâ‚‚ â†’ Nâ‚‚Oâ‚„. If 18.42 grams of Nâ‚‚Oâ‚„ (molar mass 92.01 g/mol) are produced, how many moles of Oâ‚‚ were reacted?
questions.explanation:
Moles Nâ‚‚Oâ‚„ = 18.42/92.01 = 0.200 mol
Mole ratio Oâ‚‚:Nâ‚‚Oâ‚„ = 2:1
Moles Oâ‚‚ = 0.200 أ— 2 = 0.400 mol

questions.question: 2Nâ‚‚ + Oâ‚‚ â†’ 2Nâ‚‚O. If 7.8 أ— 10آ²آ¹ particles of Nâ‚‚ are reacted, how many liters of Oâ‚‚ will also be reacted at STP?
questions.explanation:
Moles Nâ‚‚ = 7.8 أ— 10آ²آ¹ / 6.022 أ— 10آ²آ³ = 0.01296 mol
Mole ratio Nâ‚‚:Oâ‚‚ = 2:1
Moles Oâ‚‚ = 0.01296/2 = 0.00648 mol
Volume at STP = 0.00648 أ— 22.4 = 0.145 L

questions.question: Zn + 2HCl â†’ ZnClâ‚‚ + Hâ‚‚. If 0.080 moles of HCl are reacted with 0.90 moles of Zn, how many moles of ZnClâ‚‚ will be produced?
questions.explanation:
Need HCl for 0.90 mol Zn = 1.80 mol (have 0.080 mol) â†’ HCl limiting
ZnClâ‚‚ produced = 0.080/2 = 0.040 mol
